Name: Liquid Staking Centralization
Description: Network effects drive stake toward largest liquid staking providers; Lido reached 33% threshold
Trajectory: Worsening - Lido at 28-32%, trending toward supermajority
Early Warning Signs: Lido governance proposals to self-limit failing; new LST protocols failing to gain share
Historical Pattern: Similar to mining pool centralization in Bitcoin; economies of scale favor consolidation
Intervention Points: Protocol-level limits on single provider stake; social pressure campaigns; competing LST incentives

Name: Oracle Market Consolidation
Description: Integration costs and network effects favor incumbent; Chainlink maintained 46-58% despite competition
Trajectory: Stable but contested - Pyth growing via different model
Phase Transition Triggers: New chain launches choosing alternatives; major protocol switching costs dropping
Early Warning Signs: Chainlink price feeds becoming single point of failure for major protocols; no viable backup
Historical Pattern: Search engine market (Google 90%+); social networks; infrastructure tends toward monopoly
Intervention Points: Early-stage chain relationships; differentiated oracle models (pull vs push); specialized verticals

Name: ZK Prover Decentralization
Description: Currently centralized (protocol teams run provers) but technical path to decentralization exists
Trajectory: Improving - proving costs dropping; decentralized prover networks emerging
Phase Transition Triggers: Proof generation becoming commodity; multiple competitive prover implementations
Early Warning Signs: Prover networks launching (Gevulot, Succinct); proof aggregation reducing costs
Historical Pattern: Mining decentralized then recentralized; similar pattern possible
Intervention Points: Invest in prover network infrastructure; support multiple ZK implementations

Name: L2 Sequencer Roadmaps
Description: All major L2s launched with centralized sequencers; decentralization 'on roadmap' for years
Trajectory: Uncertain - roadmaps exist but timelines slip consistently
Phase Transition Triggers: Shared sequencer production launch; first L2 achieving decentralized sequencing
Early Warning Signs: Roadmap delays without explanation; revenue model dependent on sequencer fees; team resistance to timeline commitments
Historical Pattern: Ethereum went from Vitalik-controlled to decentralized; took 7+ years
Intervention Points: Shared sequencer investment; based rollup development; credible commitment mechanisms

Name: Stablecoin Regulatory Capture
Description: Regulated stablecoins increasingly dominating; USDC/USDT complying with sanctions and freezing
Trajectory: Worsening - MiCA and GENIUS Act increase compliance requirements
Phase Transition Triggers: Major jurisdiction banning non-compliant stablecoins; banking system refusing to service non-KYC stables
Early Warning Signs: Circle adding more compliance features; decentralized stablecoin market share declining; regulatory language tightening
Historical Pattern: Traditional finance pattern: money market funds captured post-2008
Intervention Points: Decentralized stablecoin scaling; non-USD stablecoin development; regulatory arbitrage jurisdictions

Name: DAO Voter Apathy
Description: Initial enthusiasm gives way to voter fatigue; participation rates declining across major DAOs
Phase Transition Triggers: Quorum thresholds consistently not met; governance attacks succeeding due to low turnout
Early Warning Signs: Proposal quality declining; delegate concentration increasing; community sentiment shifting to 'governance theater'
Historical Pattern: Shareholder democracy has same pattern; HOA voting; union elections
Intervention Points: Voting incentive mechanisms; delegation improvements; governance minimization

Name: DeFi Institutional Capture
Description: DeFi protocols adding KYC pools and compliance features to attract institutional capital
Trajectory: Accelerating - institutional demand driving compliance features
Phase Transition Triggers: Aave Arc-style permissioned pools becoming majority of TVL; non-compliant DeFi marginalized
Early Warning Signs: Major protocols launching KYC-gated products; institutional TVL exceeding retail; compliance-as-a-service integrations
Historical Pattern: Traditional finance started permissionless (bucket shops) then regulated into current form
Intervention Points: Maintain permissionless versions; fork rights; credible neutrality commitments

Name: Bridge Security Evolution
Description: After $2.8B in hacks, bridge security models evolving from multisig toward ZK proofs
Trajectory: Improving - ZK bridges in development; optimistic bridges adding fraud proofs
Phase Transition Triggers: First major ZK bridge reaching $1B+ TVL with clean security record
Early Warning Signs: ZK bridge testnets launching; legacy bridge volume declining to ZK alternatives; major hacks accelerating transition
Historical Pattern: Similar to exchange security evolution after Mt. Gox
Intervention Points: ZK bridge development; light client research; native rollup bridging

Name: MEV Democratization
Description: MEV extraction increasingly captured by sophisticated actors; some returning to users via protocols
Trajectory: Uncertain - MEV redistribution protocols emerging but builders still concentrated
Phase Transition Triggers: Encrypted mempool adoption reaching majority; inclusion lists enforced at protocol level
Early Warning Signs: Block builder concentration decreasing; MEV-Share/MEV Blocker adoption; user-facing MEV protection becoming default
Historical Pattern: Email spam: initially extractive, then filtering/defense improved
Intervention Points: MEV protection protocol investment; encrypted mempool development; user education

Name: Identity Standards Fragmentation
Description: 200+ DID methods creating fragmentation; risk of winner-take-all vs interoperability
Trajectory: Uncertain - could consolidate around winner or achieve interop
Phase Transition Triggers: Major platform adopting single standard; government identity systems choosing winner
Early Warning Signs: Cross-method verification becoming harder; developers choosing single-stack; attestation portability failing
Historical Pattern: Browser wars eventually reached standards; social login fragmented (FB/Google/Apple)
Intervention Points: Interoperability protocol investment; standards body participation; avoid single-stack lock-in

Name: Privacy Regulatory Pressure
Description: Increasing regulatory hostility to privacy technologies; Tornado Cash precedent
Phase Transition Triggers: Major ZK project receiving similar treatment to Tornado Cash; privacy features becoming liability for protocols
Early Warning Signs: Exchanges delisting privacy coins; ZK projects adding compliance features; legal defense fund activity
Historical Pattern: Encryption wars of 1990s; eventually resolved in favor of encryption but took decade
Intervention Points: Legal defense funding; regulatory engagement; privacy-preserving compliance research

Name: Quadratic Funding Sybil Arms Race
Description: Sybil attacks grow more sophisticated; defense mechanisms improve but attackers adapt
Trajectory: Ongoing - each round reveals new attack vectors and defenses
Phase Transition Triggers: Defense costs exceeding value distributed; legitimate participants excluded by false positives
Early Warning Signs: Matching pool returns diminishing; legitimate projects avoiding QF; attack sophistication increasing faster than defense
Historical Pattern: Spam/anti-spam evolution; ad fraud; any system with economic incentive to game
Intervention Points: Novel identity mechanisms; reputation staking; complementary funding mechanisms

Name: NFT Market Maturation
Description: Speculative bubble collapsed; transitioning to utility-focused applications
Trajectory: Stabilizing - down 90%+ from peak but finding sustainable niches
Early Warning Signs: credentials) reaching mainstream; speculation decoupling from utility
Historical Pattern: Audius/ticketing adoption independent of NFT price speculation; creator adoption for genuine utility
Intervention Points: Dot-com bust followed by sustainable tech companies; most speculation dies but core value survives
column11: Utility NFT applications; infrastructure vs speculation distinction

Name: Account Abstraction Adoption
Description: EOAs (externally owned accounts) being supplemented/replaced by smart contract wallets
Trajectory: Improving - ERC-4337 live; wallet adoption growing
Phase Transition Triggers: Major wallet defaulting to smart accounts; gas sponsorship becoming common
Early Warning Signs: New user onboarding increasingly via smart accounts; social recovery as default option
Historical Pattern: Mobile apps replaced desktop; took 5-10 years for full transition
Intervention Points: Smart wallet development; user experience research; gas sponsorship infrastructure
